articles.title: Severe heatwave scorches Telangana, mercury crosses 45°C - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: Five districts in Telangana recorded severe heatwave conditions on Monday, with temperatures crossing 45°C, even as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast light to moderate rainfall in parts of the state over the next three days.
The highest temperature was recorded at Kotgiri in Nizamabad district at 45.6°C, followed by Bhichkunda in Kamareddy district at 45.5°C, Pochara in Adilabad district at 45.4°C, Narsapur in Nirmal district at 45.2°C and Mallapur in Jagtial district at 45°C.
The IMD said heatwave conditions are likely to continue in some northern districts for the next two days. It also forecast isolated light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds with speeds of 40-50 kmph over the next three days.
Meteorological officials said the southwest monsoon advanced further on Monday into parts of the southeast Arabian Sea, Comorin region, southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, most parts of the Andaman Sea, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, and some parts of the central Bay of Bengal. Conditions remain favourable for further advancement into more parts of the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal over the next three to four days.
A trough extending from west Madhya Pradesh to south interior Karnataka through Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada and north interior Karnataka has formed at an altitude of 1.5 km above mean sea level. In addition, a cyclonic circulation developed over the south coastal Andhra Pradesh region between 3.1 km and 5.8 km above mean sea level, influencing weather conditions in the region.
Despite the possibility of scattered rainfall, the IMD warned that maximum temperatures in Telangana are likely to rise gradually by another 2-3°C over the next three days, increasing heat stress in several areas.

articles.title: Telangana Heatwave Alert: IMD Issues Red Alert For Several Districts - Sakshi Post
articles.full_text:
Telangana Heatwave: Red Alert For Several Districts
People across Telangana are continuing to face intense heatwave conditions as temperatures soar well above normal levels. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that the extreme weather is likely to continue for the next four days, with several districts placed under a red alert due to severe heatwave conditions.
According to weather officials, maximum temperatures in many parts of the state are being recorded nearly 4 degrees Celsius above seasonal averages, increasing the risk of heat-related illnesses.
Red Alert Issued In Multiple Districts
The IMD has sounded a red alert for districts witnessing extremely high temperatures, including Adilabad, Jagtial, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nalgonda, Peddapalli, Rajanna Sircilla and Nizamabad.
Authorities have advised people in these districts to avoid unnecessary outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours and stay hydrated.
Kotagiri Records Highest Temperature
On Monday, the highest temperature in the state was recorded at Kotagiri in Nizamabad district, where the mercury touched a scorching 45.6 degrees Celsius. The intense heat has disrupted daily life, with residents struggling to cope with rising temperatures.
Health officials have urged children, elderly people, and those with medical conditions to take extra precautions during the ongoing heatwave.
Southwest Monsoon Advances Further
Even as the state battles extreme heat, the southwest monsoon continues to make progress over several regions. The Meteorological Department stated that monsoon winds have advanced into parts of the southeast Arabian Sea, southwest and southeast Bay of Bengal, most areas of the Andaman Sea, and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
The monsoon has also entered some parts of the central Bay of Bengal, indicating gradual seasonal movement toward the Indian mainland.
Weather Systems Active Over Southern States
Meteorologists further revealed that a surface trough has formed extending from western Madhya Pradesh through central Maharashtra, Marathwada, north interior Karnataka, and up to south interior Karnataka.
In addition, a cyclonic circulation developed over the south coastal Andhra Pradesh region on Monday, which could influence weather patterns in the coming days.
Experts believe these developing weather systems may eventually bring relief from the ongoing heatwave conditions in parts of Telangana and neighbouring states.

articles.title: రెడ్‌ అలర్ట్‌ - Namasthe Telangana
articles.search_term: ("ఉష్ణోగ్రత పెరుగుదల" OR "డిగ్రీల సెల్సియస్" OR "పాదరసం పెరిగింది" OR "గరిష్ట ఉష్ణోగ్రత" OR "రికార్డు ఉష్ణోగ్రత") Telangana
articles.full_text:
ఆదిలాబాద్, మే 18(నమస్తే తెలంగాణ) : ఆదిలాబాద్ జిల్లాలో భానుడి ప్రతాపం కొనసాగుతున్నది. సోమవారం 44.6 డిగ్రీల సెల్సియస్ ఉష్ణోగ్రత నమోదు కాగా.. ప్రజలు ఉక్కిరిబిక్కిరి అవుతున్నారు. ఉదయం 8 గంటలకు ప్రారంభమైన ఎండ తీవ్రత.. సాయంత్రం ఆరు గంటల వరకు కొనసాగుతున్నది. ఎండకు వడగాలులు తోడవడంతో జనం ఉక్కిరిబిక్కిరి అవుతున్నారు. జిల్లా కేంద్రంలోని ప్రధాన రహదారులు జనసంచారం లేక నిర్మానుష్యంగా మారుతున్నాయి.
ఎండ తీవ్రత నుంచి ఉపశమనం పొందడానికి ప్రజలు గొడుగులు, టోపీలు, తలకు టవల్స్ చుట్టుకుంటున్నారు. మంచినీరు, మజ్జిగ, లస్సీ ఇతర చల్లని పానీయాలు తీసుకుంటున్నారు. మరో నాలుగు రోజులపాటు ఎండ తీవ్రత ఉంటుందని వాతావరణ శాఖ హెచ్చరించింది. ఈ నేపథ్యంలో ప్రజలు జాగ్రత్తగా ఉండాలని.. ఉదయం, సాయంత్రం పనులు చేసుకోవాలని వైద్యులు సూచిస్తున్నారు.
